title: Intervention Letter
entryBody: A guided three-paragraph structure — taken directly from Matt's intervention handout. Brevity is critical. One side of a single page.

p1.title: Why You Are Here
p1.coachNote: This paragraph exists so they know this meeting is care and concern — not punishment, blame, or shame.
p1.prompt: What do you love about them that addiction hasn't erased?
p1.placeholder: Their kindness, humor, loyalty — name what's still true. What moment shows who they really are?
p1.memoryPlaceholder: A moment of them at their best, or the relationship at its strongest.

p2.title: How This Has Affected You
p2.coachNote: The emotional impact you describe exists because of your love and investment in the relationship.
p2.maxReached: 3 experiences maximum — in the room, shorter is stronger.
p2.hints: When you lied to me about ___, I felt ___. · When you drove impaired, I felt scared. · When I see how this affects our family, I feel helpless.
p2.toneFlag: Tone check: try one specific moment and how it made you feel.

p3.title: The Request and the Boundary
p3.requestNote: This is not an invitation to negotiate alternatives.
p3.hopePlaceholder: What I hope our relationship can become if you accept help…
p3.supportPlaceholder: I'll drive you to meetings, spend time with you sober…
p3.boundariesNote: Only include boundaries you are genuinely prepared to follow through on.
p3.boundaryConfirmLabel: I will follow through on this.
p3.noBoundaries: Add anchored boundaries in the Boundaries tab first, then return here.

preview.title: Your letter
preview.overflowWarning: Your letter must fit on one side of a single page. In the room, shorter is stronger.
preview.referralCard: A letter like this is most powerful read with professional support in the room.

defaults.openerLabel: Your substance use has affected me in the following ways.
defaults.request: I want you to choose to go to treatment today.
defaults.closing: Do you see that you need help, and are you willing to accept it today?
